description.en: A named tuple giving parameters of the numeric hash implementation. For more details about hashing of numeric types, see Hashing of numeric types. hash_info.width¶ The width in bits used for hash values hash_info.modulus¶ The prime modulus P used for numeric hash scheme hash_info.inf¶ The hash value returned for a positive infinity hash_info.nan¶ (This attribute is no longer used) hash_info.imag¶ The multiplier used for the imaginary part of a complex number hash_info.algorithm¶ The name of t...

description.en: The version number encoded as a single integer. This is guaranteed to increase with each version, including proper support for non-production releases. For example, to test that the Python interpreter is at least version 1.5.2, use: if sys.hexversion >= 0x010502F0: # use some advanced feature ... else: # use an alternative implementation or warn the user ... This is called hexversion since it only really looks meaningful when viewed as the result of passing it to the built-in hex() function. ...
